Politics.headline: Trump Extends Deadline for Iran to Open Strait of Hormuz by 10 Days
Politics.detail: President Trump has extended the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by 10 days, warning of further assassinations of senior officials if the strait remains closed. Tensions remain high in the region amidst ongoing military actions.

Politics.headline: Canada Now Spending 2% of GDP on Defence, Meeting NATO Target
Politics.detail: Canada is now spending 2% of its GDP on defence, finally meeting the NATO target. However, a Conservative defence critic argues that reaching the target doesn't necessarily mean Canada is any stronger.

Politics.headline: Argentina Declares Jalisco New Generation Cartel a 'Terrorist' Group
Politics.detail: Argentina has officially declared the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, a notorious Mexican drug cartel, as a terrorist group. This move underscores the escalating concerns over transnational organized crime and its impact on national security.

Politics.headline: BJP Reports Record Donations Exceeding All Other National Parties
Politics.detail: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) in India has reported record donations, exceeding all other national parties, highlighting the evolving landscape of political financing in the country. The Prudent Electoral Trust emerged as the largest donor, contributing a total of ₹2,413.465 crore to the BJP, Congress, and AAP combined.

Politics.headline: New Trade Office to Boost Public-Private Ties with Allies: Diplomat
Politics.detail: Taiwan is launching a new trade promotion office under the Ministry of Foreign Affairs to foster public-private cooperation and create business opportunities with its diplomatic allies. The office will be modeled on the Central America Trade Office (CATO) and aims to promote economic development among Taiwan's allies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Rachel Reeves urged to raise taxes on companies profiting from war on Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: Amid the ongoing conflict, pressure is mounting on Chancellor Rachel Reeves to increase taxes on corporations benefiting from the war with Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks today: Dow Jones slumps 470 points, Nadsaq, S&P over 1% as Iran war uncertainty spooks investors.
Finance & Economy.detail: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 470 points, while the Nasdaq and S&P 500 experienced declines of over 1% due to investor concerns surrounding the US-Israeli war with Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Wall Street sees worst drop since Iran war began, as Nasdaq sinks 10% below its record.
Finance & Economy.detail: The stock market experienced its most significant decline since the beginning of the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, with the Nasdaq dropping 10% below its previous record.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Futures Rise as Trump Extends Iran Negotiations
Finance & Economy.detail: US stock futures increased after President Trump extended the deadline for striking Iranian energy infrastructure by 10 days, amidst ongoing negotiations with Tehran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Population growth in the U.S. is slowing.
Finance & Economy.detail: Recent data indicates a deceleration in population growth within the United States.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping threat actors automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating threat.

Technology.headline: Reddit Intensifies Bot Crackdown, Fishy Accounts Must Now Prove They’re Human
Technology.detail: Reddit is cracking down on bots and requiring accounts that seem suspicious to verify they are human.

Technology.headline: Wikipedia Bans AI-Generated Content, With Only Two Exceptions
Technology.detail: Wikipedia has banned AI-generated content with very few exceptions.

Technology.headline: Tiny LED design could power next-generation technology
Technology.detail: A new LED design may power future tech. The design is very small.

Technology.headline: Washington state needs a 'coherent' story to compete in AI, leaders agree
Technology.detail: Leaders in Washington state agree that a clear and unified strategy is needed to effectively compete in the rapidly evolving field of Artificial Intelligence.

Science.headline: China unveils major RISC-V achievements, opens new path for open-source chip industry
Science.detail: China has announced significant progress in RISC-V technology, potentially paving the way for advancements in the open-source chip industry.

Science.headline: Rare sperm whale birth and coordinated caregiving revealed by drone footage and underwater audio
Science.detail: Drone footage and underwater audio have captured a rare sperm whale birth, revealing insights into the coordinated caregiving behaviors of these marine mammals.

Science.headline: Extra 11 minutes' sleep each night can reduce heart attack risk, study finds
Science.detail: Research suggests that getting just 11 minutes of extra sleep each night may contribute to a reduction in the risk of heart attacks.

Science.headline: Canadian orangutan scientist Biruté Galdikas dead at 79
Science.detail: Biruté Galdikas, a renowned Canadian scientist known for her work with orangutans, has passed away at the age of 79.

Science.headline: Neanderthals present at same Siberian cave 10,000 years apart were distant relatives, 110,000-year-old bone reveals
Science.detail: DNA extracted from a Neanderthal bone fragment found in Denisova Cave reveals that Neanderthals present at the same cave 10,000 years apart were distant relatives, shedding light on the small and isolated nature of their groups.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover “Overflow Valve” in Cells Linked to Parkinson's Disease
Health.detail: Researchers have identified a crucial ion channel, TMEM175, that acts like an overflow valve in the cell's recycling system, regulating acidity inside lysosomes to ensure they function properly.

Health.headline: Doctors' pay: 3.5% award for 2026 is a “crushing blow,” leaders say
Health.detail: Medical leaders are expressing strong disapproval of the 3.5% pay raise for doctors in 2026, calling it a crushing blow.

Health.headline: Higher Meat Intake, Lower Dementia Risk — But Only in APOE4
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that higher meat intake was linked to a lower risk of dementia in individuals carrying the APOE4 gene, suggesting the potential for genotype-specific dietary recommendations.

Health.headline: Breakdown products from 'eco-friendly' plastics impede fetal development in mice, study shows
Health.detail: Research indicates that breakdown products from supposedly 'eco-friendly' plastics can negatively impact fetal development in mice.

Health.headline: Abortion: More US women using telehealth to access care amid bans
Health.detail: Due to increasing restrictions on abortion, more women in the US are turning to telehealth services to access abortion care.

Sports.headline: Mike Trout homers to kick off what he hopes will be a healthy 2026 as Angels blank Astros 3-0
Sports.detail: Mike Trout started the season strong with a home run as the Angels defeated the Astros 3-0 in their opening game.

Sports.headline: Ireland player Sammie Szmodics stretchered off after heavy collision in World Cup playoff game
Sports.detail: Sammie Szmodics was stretchered off the field following a significant collision during a World Cup playoff game.

Sports.headline: Buffalo Bills sign 3 players, including veteran interior offensive linemen Corbett and Cushenberry
Sports.detail: The Buffalo Bills have added depth to their roster by signing three players, including experienced offensive linemen Corbett and Cushenberry.

Sports.headline: Mike Trout homers to power Angels to season-opening win over Astros
Sports.detail: Mike Trout's home run propelled the Angels to a victory over the Astros in their season opener.

Sports.headline: Gyökeres hits hat-trick as Sweden and Potter see off Ukraine
Sports.detail: Viktor Gyökeres scored a hat-trick in a match where Sweden, managed by Graham Potter, defeated Ukraine.

Entertainment.headline: SHINee's Taemin to release first English-language single 'Long Way Home'
Entertainment.detail: Singer Taemin of boy band SHINee will release his first English-language digital single, “Long Way Home,” on Friday, his first project since joining the agency Galaxy Corporation earlier this month. The new track is a dreamlike pop R&B song about the sense of longing and melancholy, according to his agency.

Entertainment.headline: Stephen Colbert to write new Lord of the Rings film after end of the Late Show
Entertainment.detail: Stephen Colbert is set to write a new Lord of the Rings movie after the end of his late-night TV show. He plans to adapt six early 'Fellowship' chapters left out of the films.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Maher to receive Kennedy Center's Mark Twain prize after White House denial
Entertainment.detail: Comedian Bill Maher will receive the 2026 Mark Twain Prize for American Humor, which the Kennedy Center confirmed after an initial denial from the White House. The award gala is scheduled for June 28, preceding the Kennedy Center's two-year renovation.

Entertainment.headline: Jamie Lee Curtis to lead Murder, She Wrote reboot movie
Entertainment.detail: Jamie Lee Curtis is set to star in a reboot movie of Murder, She Wrote. This comes as a new take on the classic television series.

Entertainment.headline: Lōemis is warming up for winter with its exciting programme release
Entertainment.detail: The Lōemis Festival returns to Wellington this winter, showcasing an ambitious program of events including music, food, immersive art, spoken word, and film. Running from June 9-21, the festival will feature artists from the UK, USA, Palestine, Japan, and Aotearoa.

Crime & Justice.headline: Veteran, 83, allegedly shoved onto NYC subway tracks dies from injuries; illegal migrant charged with murder
Crime & Justice.detail: Richard Williams, an 83-year-old retired Air Force veteran, died after being randomly shoved onto NYC subway tracks by an illegal immigrant, DHS says, leading to a murder charge against the suspect.

Crime & Justice.headline: Judge lets ex-police officer walk after she denied pointing gun at fellow cop who shot her in home standoff
Crime & Justice.detail: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover officer, was found not guilty of assault after testifying she was attempting suicide, not shooting at a cop during a home standoff where she was shot.

Crime & Justice.headline: North Carolina woman who vanished 24 years ago tearfully reunites with daughter outside court
Crime & Justice.detail: A North Carolina woman missing for 24 years was found alive and tearfully reunited with her daughter outside court after appearing on a 2001 DWI charge.

Crime & Justice.headline: Hearing underway for Florida Democratic congresswoman accused of stealing FEMA funds
Crime & Justice.detail: Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, D-Fla., is facing accusations of allegedly stealing $5 million in FEMA funds, with a hearing currently underway.

Crime & Justice.headline: 2 hurt in separate Minneapolis stabbings just an hour apart, police say
Crime & Justice.detail: Minneapolis police are investigating two stabbings that occurred about an hour apart on Monday night, leaving two people injured.

Education.headline: Provincial Government’s Plan to “Protect Ontario” Fails to Address the Critical Needs of PSE and Students
Education.detail: The Canadian Federation of Students-Ontario is disappointed in the deprioritization of education in the 2026 Ontario Budget, as the funding promised falls drastically short of the sector's current needs, leaving Ontario in last place for per-student funding.

Education.headline: Ontario professors say budget short-changes universities
Education.detail: The Ontario Confederation of University Faculty Associations (OCUFA) stated that the 2026 Provincial Budget neglects Ontario's universities, faculty, students, and families because it does not provide adequate, stable funding.

Education.headline: Beyond Leadership: SoH Student Leaders Celebrate Community and Commitment
Education.detail: The School of Humanities (SoH) at NTU Singapore hosted its Student Leaders' Appreciation Night on March 25, 2026, to recognize the dedication and contributions of student leaders from the SoH Club and its five Sub-Clubs, celebrating their efforts in fostering a vibrant student community.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education's Office for Civil Rights Issues Letter of Impending Enforcement to San Jose State University on Title IX Compliance
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education's Office for Civil Rights issued a Letter of Impending Enforcement Action to San Jose State University for its ongoing refusal to comply with Title IX.

Education.headline: EdWeek: U.S. Department of Education Will Move Out of Its Longtime Offices
Education.detail: U.S. Secretary of Education Linda McMahon announced that the U.S. Department of Education will move out of the LBJ headquarters to save taxpayer money and reduce the federal education bureaucracy.

Lifestyle.headline: Lyft to launch nationwide fuel savings program as drivers feel pinch from rising gas prices
Lifestyle.detail: Lyft is introducing a fuel savings program to help its drivers cope with the increasing cost of gasoline across the country.

Lifestyle.headline: We're going back to the Moon to 'fulfill a promise' to American people: NASA administrator
Lifestyle.detail: The NASA administrator discusses the agency's plans to return to the moon and its significance for the American people.

Lifestyle.headline: Drivers told to act now as major cost is 'at its lowest for a while'
Lifestyle.detail: Drivers are being advised to take advantage of current low costs in one area, as it may not last for long.

Lifestyle.headline: 10 Minutes South Indian Style Oats Upma Recipes For A Healthy Breakfast
Lifestyle.detail: Quick and easy South Indian style oats upma recipes for a healthy and nutritious breakfast ready in just 10 minutes.

Lifestyle.headline: 3 Easy To Make Protein Rich Poha Recipes Perfect For Weight Loss
Lifestyle.detail: These poha recipes are not only delicious but also packed with protein, making them ideal for those aiming to lose weight.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Reviving pointers for good governance and realistic development goals
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discussing ways to improve governance and set achievable goals for development.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Expanding leadership opportunities for Marikina women councilors
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article likely discusses initiatives or programs aimed at increasing the involvement of women in leadership roles within the Marikina city council.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on a recovering NHS: public confidence has risen, but not enough
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the state of the National Health Service (NHS) in the UK, noting an increase in public confidence but suggesting further improvements are needed.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on China and Iran: the war poses bigger questions for Beijing than where to get its oil
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial analyzes the implications of the war involving Iran for China, suggesting that the conflict raises broader strategic concerns for Beijing beyond just oil supplies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Israel's war in Lebanon: allies must not accept a repeat of the crimes in Gaza
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial likely discusses the ongoing conflict and urges allies to not allow similar alleged crimes to be repeated.

World.headline: Trump Extends Deadline for Iran to Open Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: President Trump has once again extended the deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for oil tankers, amid ongoing negotiations. Israel has claimed responsibility for killing an Iranian naval commander overseeing the blockade.

World.headline: Russia and Ukraine Trade Attacks Ahead of U.S. Talks
World.detail: Four people have been killed as Russia and Ukraine exchange attacks, escalating tensions before planned talks in the U.S. Russia reports shooting down nearly 400 Ukrainian drones as both sides increase aerial barrages.

World.headline: Italy's Culture Minister to Increase Art Acquisitions
World.detail: Italy's Culture Ministry recently showcased a newly acquired Renaissance painting and announced plans to further increase acquisitions of significant artworks. The ministry recently purchased Antonello da Messina's 'Ecce Homo' for $14.9 million.

World.headline: UNICEF Investigates Allegations of Smuggled Tobacco in Gaza Aid Shipment
World.detail: UNICEF has launched an investigation into allegations made by Israel regarding the smuggling of tobacco within an aid shipment intended for Gaza. The investigation comes amid ongoing scrutiny of aid deliveries and restrictions in the region.

World.headline: Canada Hits NATO's 2% Defence Spending Target
World.detail: Canada has reached NATO's target of spending 2% of its GDP on defence for the first time since 1990. Prime Minister Carney announced major defence investments coming to the Maritimes.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ warns of inflation uptick as fuel price hike bites
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has cautioned about a potential increase in inflation due to rising fuel costs, impacting various sectors.

Zimbabwe.headline: Rising fuel costs hammer tourism industry
Zimbabwe.detail: The tourism sector in Zimbabwe is facing difficulties as a result of the increasing fuel prices, which are driving up operational costs.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa silent in ConCourt term-extension challenge
Zimbabwe.detail: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has not yet commented on the legal challenge against the proposed constitutional amendment that could extend his term in office.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim launches first tilapia value chain marketing strategy
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has introduced its first marketing strategy for the tilapia value chain, aiming to boost the aquaculture sector.

Zimbabwe.headline: Passion Java's Sister Hit With Fresh Charges In Which A Car She Hired Ended Up Being Sold
Zimbabwe.detail: Prophet Passion Java's sister is facing new charges related to a car she hired that was later sold under unclear circumstances.
